nodes.title: [2023-02-10] BNCT is a form of cancer treatment involving low-energy thermal neutrons
nodes.description: Boron Neutron Capture Therapy (BNCT) is a cancer treatment that uses low-energy thermal neutrons to destroy cancer cells.

nodes.title: [2023-02-10] Patient preparation involves administering a boron-containing compound
nodes.description: In the implementation of BNCT, patient preparation includes giving the patient a boron-containing compound that selectively accumulates in the cancer cells.

nodes.title: [2023-02-10] Patient is exposed to low-energy thermal neutrons during BNCT
nodes.description: The patient undergoing BNCT is exposed to a beam of low-energy thermal neutrons, which causes boron-10 atoms to capture a neutron and emit particles that destroy cancer cells.

nodes.title: [2023-02-10] Dosimetry is required for accurate dosing in BNCT
nodes.description: To ensure accurate dosing in BNCT, the patient's exposure to the neutron beam and the distribution of boron within the body must be carefully measured.

nodes.title: [2023-02-10] Quality control procedures are essential for BNCT treatment
nodes.description: Quality control procedures are in place to ensure the safe and effective delivery of BNCT, including regular maintenance and calibration of the neutron beam and equipment.

nodes.title: [2023-02-10] Patient monitoring is conducted during BNCT treatment
nodes.description: Patients undergoing BNCT are monitored throughout the treatment process to assess their response to therapy and identify potential side effects.

nodes.title: [2023-02-10] BNCT implementation specifics vary by cancer type and patient health
nodes.description: The specifics of implementing BNCT can vary depending on the type of cancer being treated, the patient's individual health status, and the facility administering the treatment.

nodes.title: [2023-02-20] Ambiguity arises from multiple interpretations in complex systems
nodes.description: Ambiguity occurs when a situation or piece of information can be interpreted in multiple ways, leading to confusion and misunderstandings in complex systems.

nodes.title: [2023-02-20] Decision-makers must clarify assumptions to overcome ambiguity
nodes.description: To overcome ambiguity, decision-makers need to engage in open dialogue, clarify their assumptions, and seek additional information or perspectives.

nodes.title: [2023-02-20] Uncertainty refers to limits of knowledge about outcomes
nodes.description: Uncertainty is the lack of information about the probabilities of different outcomes, stemming from the complexity of the system or lack of data.

nodes.title: [2023-02-20] Decision-makers use heuristics to mitigate uncertainty
nodes.description: When faced with uncertainty, decision-makers often rely on heuristics, expert judgment, or scenario planning to inform their choices.

nodes.title: [2023-02-20] Indeterminacy is the inherent unpredictability in complex systems
nodes.description: Indeterminacy refers to the fundamental unpredictability of certain aspects of complex systems due to nonlinear dynamics and interdependencies.

nodes.title: [2023-02-20] Decision-makers must accept unpredictability due to indeterminacy
nodes.description: In the face of indeterminacy, decision-makers need to accept that some aspects are inherently unpredictable and focus on building resilience and adaptability.

nodes.title: [2023-02-20] Understanding ambiguity, uncertainty, and indeterminacy is crucial for decision-making
nodes.description: Recognizing the differences between ambiguity, uncertainty, and indeterminacy is essential for decision-makers navigating complex systems.
